Because of this there will not be
so many to attend the meeting to-
night.


The Lidgerwood taxpayers will be
present to file their protest against
the abandonment of the Lidgerwood
standpipe and the committee from
the Joint meeting of the taxpayers
and chamber of commerce, which
eliminated the water meter question
for the time being, will also be pres
emit to hand in the joint resolution.

with so much other business on hand
the council could hardly get through
with that discussion tonight.


From every side there pours into
the office of The Press communica-
tions and protests against the adop-
ton of the water commission report
by the city council.


Some of these protests are against
the water meter scheme, others
against the uncalled for extrava-
gances of the plans and others pro-
test against the propping out of the
Lidgerwood standpipe plans.


In this connection, DR.. p. S. Byrne
has the following:


Editor of The Spokane Press: 1
have read with great care the report
of the water commission, consisting
of Messrs. Otto Weile, III. w Scott,
assistant city engineer of Seattle,
and Prof. Waller, of the engineering
department of the Washington agri-
cultural college.


There are several statements in
this report that seem to me to be
wrong 1t Is recommended to aban-
don the 100000 gallon steel tank
that was to be erected in Lidgerwood
and the two reasons given are that
ii was an unnecessary expense, that
it is some 20 feet lower than the
store site. AS the Lidgerwood tank
